summary: Iteration 71: Verified Universal Key billing leakage fix. All 19 backend tests passed. Code review confirms: (1) Credit pre-checks exist before ALL AI endpoints (chats, stream-handler, diffs, assets), (2) classifyProviderError translates raw provider errors to safe user-facing messages, (3) Provider status endpoint returns instant results without making actual API calls, (4) Error responses never expose raw_error, API key details, or raw provider error strings.

issues: Supabase auth - login with test credentials did not complete (expected for Supabase-based auth without proper user setup in Supabase)

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ /app/lib/ai/errors.js: classifyProviderError correctly translates billing/auth/rate-limit errors to safe user messages
✓ /app/lib/api/routes/chats.js: Credit pre-check at lines 213-230, error handler uses classifyProviderError at lines 279-283, response uses user_message not raw_error
✓ /app/lib/api/routes/diffs.js: Credit pre-check at lines 129-143, returns 402 with friendly message for insufficient credits
✓ /app/lib/api/routes/assets.js: Credit pre-check at lines 30-43, returns 402 with friendly message, SSE error handler at lines 100-113 translates raw errors
✓ /app/lib/api/routes/public.js: Provider status endpoint (lines 18-41) only checks key existence, never pings actual APIs
✓ /app/lib/api/stream-handler.js: Credit pre-check at lines 105-131, sends credits_exhausted SSE event, classifyProviderError at lines 300-337
✓ /app/lib/credits/service.js: CREDIT_COSTS properly defined (chat_message=0.5, plan_generation=2.0, file_apply=3.0, image_generation=5.0)
✓ No raw_error field found in any API response handlers
✓ No API key details exposed in error responses

success_rate.frontend: N/A - Login requires Supabase auth setup

context_for_next_testing_agent: The billing leakage fix is verified. Key patterns: (1) All AI endpoints have credit pre-checks before calling providers, (2) classifyProviderError in /app/lib/ai/errors.js translates raw provider errors to safe messages, (3) Provider status endpoint only checks key existence without making API calls, (4) Error responses use user_message from ProviderError, never raw_error. The app uses Supabase auth - test credentials need to exist in Supabase's auth system for login to work.

frontend_tests.login_form: PASS - Email and password inputs work
frontend_tests.login_submit: INCOMPLETE - Supabase auth requires user in Supabase system

error_sanitization.classifyProviderError: ✓ Translates billing/auth/rate-limit errors to safe messages
error_sanitization.no_raw_error_in_responses: ✓ Verified - API responses use user_message only
error_sanitization.no_api_key_exposure: ✓ Verified - No API key details in error responses

provider_status.no_api_calls: ✓ Only checks key existence, never pings providers
